industry.headline: Oman Plans Four 1 GW Solar Projects by 2029
industry.summary: Oman plans to develop four 1 GW solar-based independent power projects (IPPs) from 2028 to 2029. The initiative aims to generate at least 30% of the country's electricity from renewable sources by 2030.

industry.headline: Vikram Solar Commissions N-Type Module Plant
industry.summary: Vikram Solar has commissioned a new manufacturing facility at Gangaikondan in Tamil Nadu. The plant produces Hypersol N-Type TOPCon G12R modules with 615-640 Wp output and up to 23.69% efficiency.

industry.headline: MNRE mandates ALMM List II cells for govt rooftop solar
industry.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) clarified that government rooftop solar projects commissioned after June 1, 2026, or bid post-August 31, 2025, without exemptions must use solar cells from ALMM List II. This applies to new projects under the specified timelines.

industry.headline: Delhi mandates electric two-wheelers from 2028

tech.headline: Norwegian Startup Develops Floating PV Tech for High Waves
tech.summary: Fred. Olsen 1848, a Norwegian company, has secured technology validation for its floating solar system. The technology is designed to withstand waves of up to 3.5 meters. This development focuses on creating floating photovoltaic systems capable of operating in challenging marine environments with significant wave heights.

tech.headline: New York Surpasses 8 GW of Distributed Solar Installation

tech.headline: Chinese Researchers Achieve 27.3% Efficiency in Perovskite Solar Cell
tech.summary: Chinese scientists developed a dual-molecule interfacial layer for inverted perovskite solar cells, enhancing molecular ordering and charge extraction. The optimized devices achieved 27.3% efficiency and demonstrated strong stability under long-term light soaking and outdoor testing, as reported by PV Magazine.

learn.title: Hydrogen Replacement in Steel Production
learn.intro: The transition from fossil fuels to clean energy demands innovation in critical sectors like steel manufacturing. Hydrogen's role in reducing carbon emissions has reshaped industrial priorities globally, challenging existing infrastructures and economies worldwide.
learn.c1_text: Hydrogen energy offers scalable solutions for decarbonizing heavy industries, though its adoption requires overcoming technical and economic barriers currently limiting widespread use.
learn.c2_text: As global demand surges, integrating renewable power into existing grid systems demands advanced storage and distribution strategies to ensure reliability and affordability.
learn.stat1: India aims to produce 200 GW of renewable energy by 2024, a shift critical to national climate goals.
learn.stat2: Solar adoption in India has surpassed 50 GW capacity, demonstrating rapid scalability.

learn.title: Geothermal Energy Potential
learn.intro: Geothermal energy harnesses Earth's internal heat to generate electricity and heat buildings, offering a stable, low-emission alternative to fossil fuels with minimal land disruption.
learn.c1_text: Geothermal energy provides consistent baseload power, capable of supplying 10-15% of global electricity needs while emitting little to no greenhouse gases.
learn.c2_text: Countries like Iceland leverage geothermal reserves to achieve nearly 100% renewable electricity, showcasing viable scalability.
learn.stat1: The United States has 10 GW of active geothermal capacity, contributing significantly to its clean energy portfolio.
learn.stat2: Thermal efficiency in geothermal systems exceeds 90%, making it highly efficient.

learn.title: India's EV Policy Challenges
learn.intro: India's push for electric vehicles aligns with its net-zero goals but faces hurdles like charging infrastructure gaps and cost disparities between urban and rural areas.
learn.c1_text: While EV adoption grows, adoption rates lag in regions lacking reliable charging networks and affordable pricing compared to internal combustion engines.
learn.c2_text: Urban centers benefit from EVs' efficiency, yet rural areas struggle with limited infrastructure to support widespread uptake.
learn.stat1: India plans to introduce 4 million EVs by 2030, aiming to curb pollution in cities.
learn.stat2: Government subsidies reduce costs but raise concerns about long-term sustainability.

learn.title: Carbon Markets Mechanics
learn.intro: Carbon markets aim to incentivize low-carbon practices by assigning financial value to emissions reductions, creating a complex yet vital framework for global compliance.
learn.c1_text: Carbon credits enable businesses to offset emissions cost-effectively, though their efficacy depends on transparent regulatory standards.
learn.c2_text: Traders invest in projects promising carbon credits, balancing profit motives with environmental impact.
learn.stat1: The EU ETS has driven 40% of emissions reductions since 2005, demonstrating market impact.
learn.stat2: Volatility in carbon prices creates uncertainty for long-term planning.

learn.title: Offshore Wind Expansion
learn.intro: Offshore wind farms leverage coastal areas for high-capacity energy generation, addressing land-use conflicts while harnessing consistent winds.
learn.c1_text: Global offshore wind capacity reached 30 GW in 2023, offering a pathway to avoid land-use disputes.
learn.c2_text: Coastal nations like Denmark integrate offshore wind into coastal economies without displacing agriculture.
learn.stat1: Global offshore wind capacity surpassed 50 GW, supporting 15% of Europe's power needs.
learn.stat2: Installation costs remain 10x higher than onshore alternatives currently.

learn.title: Direct Air Capture (DAC)
learn.intro: Direct Air Capture captures atmospheric CO₂ directly, offering a potential solution but facing challenges in scalability and energy demands.
learn.c1_text: DAC systems can sequester 1 million tons annually, yet remain 10-20% energy-intensive, limiting feasibility.
learn.c2_text: Despite costs, advancements in materials are making DAC viable for large-scale deployment.
learn.stat1: Global DAC projects aim to remove 500 million tons of CO₂ by 2030, but require vast resources.
learn.stat2: Current costs exceed $100 per ton, hindering adoption despite climate urgency.
